status: internal vertical diagnostic pack draft, not buyer-ready final copy
standard_boundary_line: This vertical diagnostic pack is a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review extension. It is not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, runtime monitor, security tool, deployment approval, financial advice, accounting assurance, or guarantee of AI safety.
buyer_problem: Finance and reconciliation agents can appear operationally complete while acting on stale records, unresolved ledger mismatches, partial settlement state, weak approval boundaries, or unsafe rollback assumptions.
buyer_roles: Engineering lead for financial data infrastructure
Risk or governance lead evaluating finance automation

likely_failure_families.description: The agent treats one system as complete while another system remains unresolved or inconsistent.
The agent reports reconciliation, closure, payment, or exception handling as complete without independent confirmation.
The agent acts on outdated balance, payment, invoice, settlement, or ledger data as if it were current.
The agent assumes a wrong payment, posting, or ledger action can be safely reversed without checking system constraints.
The agent uses narrow human approval to justify broader financial action.
The agent treats screenshots, exported summaries, forwarded reports, or partial dashboards as decision-grade evidence.

selected_v0_5_case_types.why_selected: Finance workflows often depend on forwarded reports, exports, dashboards, and partial evidence that must not be treated as authoritative without validation.
Screenshots, spreadsheet exports, and dashboard summaries can hide unresolved transactional state.
Reconciliation and settlement workflows are highly sensitive to timestamps, batch timing, and delayed updates.
Finance agents commonly span CRM, billing, ERP, payment, bank, and ledger systems.
Premature closure of reconciliation or exception workflows can create downstream accounting and operational risk.
Financial actions may not be safely reversible even when an agent assumes rollback is available.
Narrow approval for one exception can be incorrectly generalized to larger financial actions.

safe_use_cases: Architecture-stage review of a finance or reconciliation agent before broader rollout.
Pre-deployment review of how an agent handles stale, partial, or conflicting financial records.
Review of handoff points between billing, payments, ERP, ledger, and human finance teams.
Review of escalation thresholds for financial exceptions, unresolved matches, or ambiguous completion states.
Internal design-support review for engineering, finance operations, product, and risk teams.
excluded_uses: Not legal, tax, or financial advice.
Not a guarantee that financial workflows are safe, accurate, compliant, or deployment-ready.
Not a replacement for internal finance, accounting, legal, risk, or audit teams.
deliverables: State mismatch and false-completion risk map
Evidence, provenance, and proxy-handling gap list
Approval, handoff, escalation, and recovery-path review

green: Buyer has one concrete finance or reconciliation workflow.
Workflow touches multiple systems or handoffs.
Silent failure would create meaningful financial, operational, trust, or rework cost.
Buyer can provide bounded non-production workflow context.
Buyer understands this is diagnostic, not audit or compliance sign-off.
yellow: Workflow exists but system boundaries are unclear.
Buyer has broad finance automation concerns but no single workflow selected.
Buyer wants a review but cannot yet provide enough workflow context.
red: Buyer wants financial audit, accounting assurance, compliance certification, fraud detection, runtime monitoring, legal/tax advice, or deployment approval.
Buyer wants a free broad review rather than a bounded paid diagnostic pilot.
Buyer expects guaranteed prevention of financial errors.

claim_boundary: This pack may be described only as a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review extension for finance/reconciliation agent workflows. It must not be described as financial audit, compliance certification, accounting assurance, fraud detection, runtime monitoring, financial advice, or deployment approval.
failure_column: This pack stops fitting if the buyer expects SpecShift to certify financial correctness, replace audit/accounting/legal review, monitor live transactions, detect fraud in production, approve deployment, or guarantee safety, compliance, or financial accuracy.
recommended_next_step: Use this pack only after a finance or reconciliation workflow is identified. Select relevant v0.5 cases manually, then generate a narrow pilot scope outline.
